name: Front Tab Relay
short_description: A menu-bar relay for grabbing the front browser tab and routing its title and URL into prompts, notes, or tickets without losing your place.
audience: Mac users who hand browser context into other tools
Researchers and operators moving links into notes or tickets
AI-assisted builders who keep bouncing between browser, chat, and docs
detail_lines: Reads the visible front tab from Safari or common Chrome-family browsers without network access.
Formats the title and URL for markdown, prompts, tickets, or plain-text notes in one step.
Keeps the relay action close to the menu bar so browser context leaves the tab strip cleanly.
